prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. In November 2014, general elections were conducted in Bahrain with the purpose of selecting the forty members who would serve on the Council of Representatives. The initial round of voting occurred on November 22nd. A subsequent second round was held on November 29th for the 34 constituencies where no single candidate secured a majority of the votes. The Shiite Islamist opposition group, Al-Wefaq, chose not to participate in these elections. Among the 266 individuals who put themselves forward as candidates, 22 were women. Election authorities reported that a total of 349,713 Bahraini citizens were registered to cast their ballots, comprising 175,998 men and 173,175 women. While the government officially stated that voter turnout reached 52.6%, the opposition contended that the actual figure was merely 30%. Independent candidates emerged victorious, securing 37 out of the 40 available seats. Sunni Islamists experienced a reduction in their representation, losing two of the five seats they previously held. Due to the boycott by Al-Wefaq, the number of Shiite Members of Parliament decreased to 14. Female representation was also impacted.
B. In November 2014, Bahrain held general elections for the Council of Representatives, with forty members elected. The first round of voting took place on 22 November, followed by a second round on 29 November in 34 constituencies where no candidate won a majority. The elections were boycotted by the Shiite Islamist opposition Al-Wefaq, with 349,713 Bahrainis listed to vote, including 175,998 men and 173,175 women, while the opposition claimed it was only 30%.
C. General elections were held in Bahrain in November 2014 to elect the forty members of the Council of Representatives. The first round of voting took place on 22 November, with a second round on 29 November in the 34 constituencies in which no candidate received a majority.The elections were boycotted by the Shiite Islamist opposition Al-Wefaq. Of the 266 candidates, 22 were women. According to election officials, 349,713 Bahrainis, including 175,998 men and 173,175 women, were listed to vote. Although the government announced the voter turnout as 52.6%, the opposition claimed it was only 30%.Independents won 37 of the 40 seats with Sunni Islamists losing two of their five seats. The number of Shiite MPs fell to 14 as a result of the Al-Wefaq boycott. Female representation was

Answer:
